commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer policy review for env-surface and local automation behavior, not a concrete automated repair.; Cleared: The diff touches a preinstall lifecycle script, but the new refusal path is opt-in, source-checkout scoped, CI-skipped, and does not add third-party code execution or dependency changes. (sha=11122c6e62b5290196e5cf97e9994f667690683e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens local heavy-check locking for type-aware oxlint and scoped tooling tests, adds an opt-in install-pressure preinstall guard, and makes adjacent cron/agent test cleanup changes.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +107, Other +127. Total +244 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can skip the heavy-check lock for explicit type-aware oxlint file targets and for single scoped tooling test runs; I did not execute local heavy checks in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer policy review for env-surface and local automation behavior, not a concrete automated repair.

target.title: fix(scripts): close local heavy-check lock gaps
target.body:
## Summary
- keep the shared local heavy-check lock for type-aware oxlint, including explicit file-target runs that can spawn `tsgolint headless`
- wrap sharded oxlint in one parent heavy-check lock while child shards skip duplicate locks to avoid self-deadlock
- stop exempting scoped tooling test runs from the shared heavy-check lock
- add an opt-in source-checkout install pressure guard so local `pnpm install` can refuse to start when memory/swap/load are already dangerous
- clean up lint-only failures on the branch, including a stale cron isolated-agent test import

## Why
A Pi incident showed `tsgolint` entering through type-aware oxlint and consuming ~5.25 GiB RSS while swap was nearly exhausted. Direct `run-tsgo` was guarded, but type-aware oxlint and scoped tooling tests could still bypass the shared lock and run beside other heavy local work.

The later post-incident sweep found one more practical pressure path: local source-checkout installs could start while the host was already choking. This PR adds an operator-controlled guard for that path without changing default install behavior.

## Behavior
- Local heavy-check serialization now covers:
  - type-aware oxlint with explicit file targets
  - sharded oxlint parent runs
  - scoped tooling test runs
- Shard children skip duplicate locking after the parent lock is acquired.
- Local source-checkout installs refuse under host pressure only when `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=1` is set and any of these are true:
  - `MemAvailable < 2GiB`
  - `SwapFree < 1GiB`
  - `load1 > 10`
- CI/GitHub Actions skip the install refusal.
- Without `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=1`, local installs keep the existing warning-only behavior.

## Test Plan
- `node --check scripts/lib/local-heavy-check-runtime.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.mjs`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_FORCE_LOCK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts --run`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/lib/local-heavy-check-runtime.mjs scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.mjs test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --threads=1`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: The local heavy-check guards now keep type-aware oxlint and sharded oxlint behind the shared local heavy-check lock, avoid duplicate child-shard locking, and add an opt-in install pressure refusal path for source checkouts.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw source checkout on Raspberry Pi host, rebased onto current `origin/main` at the time of repair. The host was under real pressure during verification (`SwapFree below 1GiB`, `load1 above 10`), which exercised the opt-in install pressure guard path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Rebased the PR branch onto current `origin/main`.
2. Ran `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main HEAD`.
3. Ran `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`.
4. Ran `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=1 pnpm test test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`, which hit the opt-in install pressure guard.
5. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` to verify the targeted behavior without dependency reconciliation in the pressured worktree.
6. Final caretaker repair at head `90ccd47af33a90ba43b4ed24a16b9abe0a7bb948` removed the stale cron isolated-agent import flagged by CI/review and ran `git diff --check` before pushing. No broad local CI was run on the Raspberry Pi; GitHub Actions is the source of truth for the full matrix.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real Raspberry Pi checkout showed the opt-in `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=1 pnpm test ...` wrapper refusing unsafe local work with `Refusing to run package-manager install/update on this host because SwapFree below 1GiB and load1 above 10`. The same terminal session then showed targeted `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` output passing 4 files / 127 tests. The final import cleanup is a direct removal of unused imports from `src/cron/isolated-agent.run-timeout-override.test.ts`, including the missing `../agents/pi-embedded.js` import that failed the cron isolated-agent CI lane.

Observed result after fix: The opt-in runtime guard refused pressured local dependency reconciliation, while the targeted Vitest command passed the affected local-heavy-check behavior without bypassing the guard. The final head no longer imports the missing cron test module, so the previous `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` blocker is addressed at source.

What was not tested: I did not run broad/full local heavy checks on the Raspberry Pi because this change intentionally avoids unsafe heavy local work under pressure. CI remains the broad proof surface for the full matrix.
target.author: andyylin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: The local heavy-check guards now keep type-aware oxlint and sharded oxlint behind the shared local heavy-check lock, avoid duplicate child-shard locking, and add an opt-in install pressure refusal path for source checkouts.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw source checkout on Raspberry Pi host, rebased onto current `origin/main` at the time of repair. The host was under real pressure during verification (`SwapFree below 1GiB`, `load1 above 10`), which exercised the opt-in install pressure guard path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Rebased the PR branch onto current `origin/main`.
2. Ran `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main HEAD`.
3. Ran `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`.
4. Ran `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=1 pnpm test test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`, which hit the opt-in install pressure guard.
5. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` to verify the targeted behavior without dependency reconciliation in the pressured worktree.
6. Final caretaker repair at head `90ccd47af33a90ba43b4ed24a16b9abe0a7bb948` removed the stale cron isolated-agent import flagged by CI/review and ran `git diff --check` before pushing. No broad local CI was run on the Raspberry Pi; GitHub Actions is the source of truth for the full matrix.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real Raspberry Pi checkout showed the opt-in `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=1 pnpm test ...` wrapper refusing unsafe local work with `Refusing to run package-manager install/update on this host because SwapFree below 1GiB and load1 above 10`. The same terminal session then showed targeted `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` output passing 4 files / 127 tests. The final import cleanup is a direct removal of unused imports from `src/cron/isolated-agent.run-timeout-override.test.ts`, including the missing `../agents/pi-embedded.js` import that failed the cron isolated-agent CI lane.

Observed result after fix: The opt-in runtime guard refused pressured local dependency reconciliation, while the targeted Vitest command passed the affected local-heavy-check behavior without bypassing the guard. The final head no longer imports the missing cron test module, so the previous `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` blocker is addressed at source.

What was not tested: I did not run broad/full local heavy checks on the Raspberry Pi because this change intentionally avoids unsafe heavy local work under pressure. CI remains the broad proof surface for the full matrix.

commands.author: andyylin

target.title: fix(scripts): close local heavy-check lock gaps
target.body:
## Summary
- keep the shared local heavy-check lock for type-aware oxlint, including explicit file-target runs that can spawn `tsgolint headless`
- wrap sharded oxlint in one parent heavy-check lock while child shards skip duplicate locks to avoid self-deadlock
- stop exempting scoped tooling test runs from the shared heavy-check lock
- add an opt-in source-checkout install pressure guard so local `pnpm install` can refuse to start when memory/swap/load are already dangerous
- clean up lint-only failures on the branch, including a stale cron isolated-agent test import

## Why
A Pi incident showed `tsgolint` entering through type-aware oxlint and consuming ~5.25 GiB RSS while swap was nearly exhausted. Direct `run-tsgo` was guarded, but type-aware oxlint and scoped tooling tests could still bypass the shared lock and run beside other heavy local work.

The later post-incident sweep found one more practical pressure path: local source-checkout installs could start while the host was already choking. This PR adds an operator-controlled guard for that path without changing default install behavior.

## Behavior
- Local heavy-check serialization now covers:
  - type-aware oxlint with explicit file targets
  - sharded oxlint parent runs
  - scoped tooling test runs
- Shard children skip duplicate locking after the parent lock is acquired.
- Local source-checkout installs refuse under host pressure only when `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=1` is set and any of these are true:
  - `MemAvailable < 2GiB`
  - `SwapFree < 1GiB`
  - `load1 > 10`
- CI/GitHub Actions skip the install refusal.
- Without `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=1`, local installs keep the existing warning-only behavior.

## Test Plan
- `node --check scripts/lib/local-heavy-check-runtime.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.mjs`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_FORCE_LOCK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts --run`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/lib/local-heavy-check-runtime.mjs scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.mjs test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --threads=1`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run`
- `git diff --check`
